hints.Mariam: 'Mariam' is a proper noun — the name Mary in the accusative
hints.Ioseph: 'Ioseph' is a proper noun — the name Joseph (indeclinable)

hints.to come: 'to come' is 'venire' — 'Invenerunt' comes from 'invenire' (in + venire = to find)
hints.to invent: English 'invent' derives from Latin 'invenire' but the primary meaning is 'to find/discover'

hints.Present: Present would be 'inveniunt' — 'Invenerunt' with -erunt ending is perfect
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'inveniebant' — 'Invenerunt' is 3rd plural perfect
hints.Future: Future would be 'invenient' — 'Invenerunt' is 3rd plural perfect

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come) — 'invenire' adds the prefix in-
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'Maria' — 'Mariam' with the -am ending is accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'Maria' — 'Mariam' is accusative, direct object of 'Invenerunt'

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'Mariae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Mariae' ends in -ae
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Mariae' ends in -ae
